stage.name: Intro to Trading (Elementary)
stage.description: Buy low, sell high — and why copy trading changes the game for beginners.

elements.content: Buying and selling to make a profit

Trading means buying something when the price is low and selling it when the price is higher — keeping the difference as profit. Example: You buy 1 XRP for $2. Later, XRP goes up to $3. You sell it. You made $1 profit — that's trading!

🛒

Buy Low

Buy when you think price will go up

⏳

Wait

Watch the market for the right moment

💰

Sell High

Sell when the price goes up

actions.text: 📈 What is Trading?
Buying and selling to make a profit

Trading means buying something when the price is low and selling it when the price is higher — keeping the difference as profit. Example: You buy 1 XRP for $2 . Later, XRP goes up to $3 . You sell it. You made $1 profit — that's trading!
🛒 Buy Low Buy when you think price will go up
⏳ Wait Watch the market for the right moment
💰 Sell High Sell when the price goes up
🎉 Keep Profit The difference is yours!

elements.content: The two forces that set every price

Every price you see in a market is the result of a negotiation between buyers and sellers. Buyers want the lowest price. Sellers want the highest. The current price is where they agree.


When buyers outnumber sellers — prices go up. When sellers outnumber buyers — prices go down. This is the foundation of every market in the world, from stocks to crypto to real estate.

🎯 Your Job
Predict which way it moves

actions.text: 📊 Buyers vs Sellers
The two forces that set every price

Every price you see in a market is the result of a negotiation between buyers and sellers . Buyers want the lowest price. Sellers want the highest. The current price is where they agree. When buyers outnumber sellers — prices go up . When sellers outnumber buyers — prices go down . This is the foundation of every market in the world, from stocks to crypto to real estate.
📈 More Buyers. Price rises
📉 More Sellers. Price falls
⚖️ Equal. Price stays flat
🎯 Your Job. Predict which way it moves

elements.content: The two ways to make money in any market

Going Long means you buy something hoping the price will go up. You buy at $100, it goes to $150 — you made $50. This is the most natural way to trade.


Going Short means you bet the price will go down. You borrow and sell at $100, price drops to $70, you buy it back — and pocket the difference.


⚠️ Shorting is advanced and risky. Losses can exceed your deposit. Start with long-only trading.

✅ Beginner Tip
Stick to long positions first

actions.text: 📈 Long vs Short — Simply Explained
The two ways to make money in any market

Going Long means you buy something hoping the price will go up . You buy at $100, it goes to $150 — you made $50. This is the most natural way to trade. Going Short means you bet the price will go down . You borrow and sell at $100, price drops to $70, you buy it back — and pocket the difference. ⚠️ Shorting is advanced and risky. Losses can exceed your deposit. Start with long-only trading.
⬆️ Long. Buy low, sell high
⬇️ Short. Sell high, buy back lower
⚠️ Risk. Shorting = unlimited loss potential
✅ Beginner Tip. Stick to long positions first

elements.content:
Volatility means how much and how fast a price moves. Bitcoin can move 10% in a single day. Apple stock might move 2% in a week.


High volatility means bigger profit potential — but also bigger loss potential. Crypto is one of the most volatile asset classes in history. This is why risk management — never investing more than you can afford to lose — is the first rule of trading.

🛡️ Rule #1
Only risk what you can lose

actions.text: 🎢 What is Volatility?
Why crypto prices move so dramatically

Volatility means how much and how fast a price moves. Bitcoin can move 10% in a single day. Apple stock might move 2% in a week. High volatility means bigger profit potential — but also bigger loss potential . Crypto is one of the most volatile asset classes in history. This is why risk management — never investing more than you can afford to lose — is the first rule of trading.
📊 High Volatility. Big moves, fast
💰 Opportunity. Big gains possible
💸 Risk. Big losses possible
🛡️ Rule #1. Only risk what you can lose

questions.question: What is the basic goal of trading?

options.label: To hold onto your money forever
To buy low and sell high to make a profit
To give money to a bank and earn interest

questions.analysis: The core of trading is simple: buy when the price is low, sell when the price is higher, and keep the difference as profit.

questions.question: What is copy trading?

options.label: Automatically mirroring an expert trader's buy and sell moves
Printing extra copies of a coin
Manually watching a trader and copying them by hand

questions.analysis: Copy trading automatically replicates an expert's trades in your account — when they buy, you buy; when they sell, you sell. No manual effort required.

questions.question: What is the golden rule of trading?

options.label: Never invest more than you can afford to lose
Trust everyone who offers trading tips online

questions.analysis: Never invest money you can't afford to lose. Even expert traders have bad days. Protecting your capital comes first.

questions.question: What does OnlyCrypto use for its copy trading?

options.label: Bitunix — a professional copy trading exchange

questions.analysis: OnlyCrypto uses Bitunix as its copy trading platform. Bitunix is a professional exchange that enables expert traders to have their trades mirrored automatically to followers. Payouts are made via the XRP Ledger.

actions.text: Time to test what you've learned! Answer each question and see how you do.
Take your time — the goal is understanding, not just getting the right answer.
